sections.paragraph:
αἷμα

I. blood, Hom., etc.; in pl. streams of blood, Trag.

II. bloodshed, murder, Aesch., etc.: —ἐφ' αἵματι φεύγειν to avoid trial for murder by going into exile, Dem.; so, αἷμα φεύγειν Eur.

III. like Lat. sanguis, blood-relationship, kin, Soph., Hom., etc.; ὁ πρὸς αἵματος one of the blood or race, Soph.; μητρὸς τῆς ἐμῆς ἐν αἵματι akin to her by blood, Aesch.


Etym. Deriv. uncertain.
